---
title: Rolling your own Neovim statusline
date: 2026-04-15
---

With the release of Neovim 0.12 I decided to finally overhaul my statusline.
For a long time this has been the one part of my config which has never felt
great.

## The default statusline

For the uninitiated, this is what the default Neovim statusline looks like:

![Default Neovim statusline](nvim-statusline-default.png)

By default you get:

*   The full path to the current buffer's file
*   The cursor position in the file (`49,29`)
*   The position of the **viewport relative to the length** of the file (`62%`)


I've been a long time user *of* the _very_ _**popular**_
[lualine](https://github.com/nvim-lualine/lualine.nvim), but creating a custom
statusline that does exactly what I want 


``` lua
local a = b + c

for i, x in pairs({a = 1, b = 2}) do
    print(i)
    print(x)
end
```
